task_create.question: Can an external client create a task?

request_fields.hide_in_task_list: boolean — default false (useful for automated tasks)
request_fields.title: string — custom title

task_create.notes: Fully documented. hide_in_task_list=true ideal for automated BUS tasks. project_id enables project instruction injection.

send_message.question: Can an external client send a message into an existing task?

request_fields.agent_profile: string — optional, does not default to manus-1.6
request_fields.structured_output_schema: JSON Schema — arms schema for next finish

send_message.notes: Critical for BUS: can relay MP packet as message to existing Manus task. agent-default-main_task shortcut eliminates task ID lookup.

file_upload.question: Can an external client upload and attach a file to a task?
flow: 1. POST /v2/file.upload with {filename} → get file_id + upload_url (presigned S3, expires 3min)
4. Use file_id in message.content ContentPart when calling task.create or task.sendMessage
file_upload.notes: Full upload flow proven. file_id method supports up to 512MB. Alternative: file_url (Manus fetches) or file_data (base64 inline).

task_attachment.question: Can an external client attach a file to a task?

methods.file_id: Upload via file.upload, attach file_id in ContentPart
methods.file_url: Pass URL directly — Manus downloads (20MB limit)

task_attachment.notes: Three attachment methods. file_id preferred for >20MB files. ContentPart array allows mixed text + files.

workspace_write.question: Can an external client write directly into the persistent workspace (/home/ubuntu/)?
workspace_write.evidence: No API endpoint exists for direct filesystem write. file.upload writes to S3 CDN (not workspace). Manus agent writes to workspace during task execution only.
workspace_write.workaround: task.create/sendMessage with instruction to write file to /home/ubuntu/yos-bus-runtime/inbox/mpm/ — Manus agent executes the write during task run.
workspace_write.notes: CRITICAL BLOCKER for direct BUS write. Indirect write via task instruction is the only path.

workspace_read.question: Can an external client read from the persistent workspace?
workspace_read.evidence: No API endpoint for direct filesystem read. task.listMessages can retrieve agent output messages and attachments (CDN URLs), but not raw filesystem files.
workspace_read.workaround: task.create/sendMessage with instruction to read file and return content — Manus agent reads and returns in assistant_message.
workspace_read.notes: Indirect read via task instruction is the only path. Structured output can return file content as JSON.

conversation_continuation.question: Can the API trigger or continue work in an existing conversation?

methods.task_sendMessage: Send follow-up to any task_id — continues execution
methods.agent_default_main_task: Shortcut for IM agent persistent conversation
methods.waiting_reply: task.sendMessage to reply when agent asks question (messageAskUser event)

conversation_continuation.notes: Full conversation continuation proven. agent-default-main_task = persistent IM agent thread. Can resume stopped tasks.

auth_model.question: What authentication model is required?

api_key.notes: Simplest. No expiry. Recommended for server-to-server (ChatGPT → Manus).

scopes.create_task: Create tasks, send messages, upload files — scoped to OAuth app
scopes.manage_all_tasks: Full access to all user tasks

oauth2.notes: For third-party apps. create_task scope isolates tasks per OAuth app.

auth_model.recommended: api_key for BUS integration (server-to-server, no user interaction required)

structured_output.question: Can the API receive structured JSON results from Manus?

retrieval.polling: task.listMessages → structured_output_result event after task stops

structured_output.schema_constraints: All object properties must be in required[], additionalProperties must be false
structured_output.notes: Excellent for MPR delivery. Manus can return MPR as structured JSON after task completion.

webhooks.question: Can the API push notifications to an external endpoint?
webhooks.notes: Ideal for last-mile MPR delivery. Eliminates polling. Requires external webhook receiver.

blocking_limitations.description: No direct workspace write API. External clients cannot write files directly to /home/ubuntu/.
blocking_limitations.workaround: task.create/sendMessage with write instruction — Manus agent executes write during task run (async, ~30-60s latency).
blocking_limitations.impact: BUS first-mile cannot be synchronous. Must accept async write pattern.

blocking_limitations.description: No direct workspace read API. External clients cannot read /home/ubuntu/ files.
blocking_limitations.workaround: task.create/sendMessage with read+return instruction. Or: structured_output to return file content as JSON.
blocking_limitations.impact: BUS last-mile cannot be synchronous. Must accept async read pattern.

blocking_limitations.description: file.upload writes to S3 CDN (not workspace). CDN URLs are public but not persistent workspace paths.
blocking_limitations.workaround: Use file_id in task message — Manus agent can access CDN file and write to workspace.
blocking_limitations.impact: file.upload alone is not sufficient for workspace write. Must combine with task instruction.

blocking_limitations.description: Rate limit: 10 task.create/min. Burst of MPs could hit limit.
blocking_limitations.workaround: Queue MPs, send one at a time with 6s spacing.

recommended_bus_integration_pattern.name: Async Task Relay Pattern
recommended_bus_integration_pattern.description: ChatGPT sends MP packet as message to Manus via task.sendMessage (agent-default-main_task). Manus agent writes packet to BUS inbox, executes MP, writes MPR to BUS outbox. ChatGPT polls task.listMessages or receives webhook for MPR.
sequence: 2. Manus agent: receives message, writes to /home/ubuntu/yos-bus-runtime/inbox/mpm/<packet>.md
3. Manus agent: executes MP, writes MPR to /home/ubuntu/yos-bus-runtime/outbox/mpm/<mpr>.md
4a. ChatGPT: polls GET /v2/task.listMessages?task_id=agent-default-main_task for assistant_message with MPR
4b. OR: webhook push on task_stopped event with structured_output MPR
recommended_bus_integration_pattern.requires: Manus agent trained to recognize MP packets and execute BUS write
